title: Year 10 Mathematics: Traditional Methods and Practices - Enhanced by Pāngarau_Kaitiaki
subject: Mathematics
culturalContext: Connecting mathematical concepts to the local environment of Mangakōtukutuku College and Ngāti Kahungunu traditions.
learningObjectives: Develop deep understanding of mathematics concepts through cultural context
Apply traditional knowledge to modern mathematics challenges
Connect learning to local environment and community
Demonstrate respect for Māori and Pacific perspectives

activities.title: Whakawhanaungatanga: Building Connections
activities.description: Begin with karakia and introductions. Students share their prior knowledge and connections to the topic.

activities.title: Cultural Context Exploration
activities.description: Students will measure and map the school grounds using traditional Māori measurement techniques alongside modern mathematical tools.

activities.title: Hands-on Learning
activities.description: Practical application of mathematics concepts using both traditional and modern methods.

activities.title: Reflection and Sharing
activities.description: Students reflect on their learning and share insights with the class, connecting to cultural values.

resources: Local kaumātua for traditional measurement knowledge

tasks: Formative: Teacher observation during activities and discussions
Summative: Student presentation demonstrating understanding and cultural connection

nzcAlignment: Te Tiriti o Waitangi: Cultural responsiveness and partnership
Key Competencies: Thinking, relating to others, using language and symbols
